events.title: TD Summer Reading Club - Book Reporting
events.description: June 20th - August 31st. Ages 0 - 13. Summer Reading Club is here ! Register your child for the club and receive a TDSRC book reporting workbook, stickers, and more to get the most out of your summer reading. Kids will be able to report 2 books per day during the book reporting times and for  every 5 books reported they will receive an entry for a grand prize ! Find fun activities in branch all summer long and online at tdsummerreadingclub.ca . This years theme is SET SAIL! Explore the worl

events.title: Scavenger Hunt: Local Ducks
events.description: Drop by Fort York Library to find local duck species hidden around the branch and win a special 3D printed prize made in Fort York's own Digital Innovation Hub! This month's scavenger hunt features illustrations of local Toronto duck species from the Special Collections in the Arts at the Toronto Reference Library, many of which can be spotted in their full feathered glory on Toronto's waterfront.

events.title: Philippe Flahaut: Sous le soleil de l'été
events.description: [English version follows] Il s’agit d’un programme de chansons traditionnelles de France et du Québec, dont le vocabulaire est axé sur les activités estivales : les vacances, l’eau, le soleil, les animaux et les couleurs. Le programme comprend des chansons à répondre, des chansons accompagnées d’instruments de musique et des jeux musicaux. C’est une expérience interactive qui encourage la participation active des enfants. Âge: préscolaires jusqu’à la troisième année, soit 3 à 12 ans This program

events.title: Family Movie: GOAT
events.description: Join us this Summer at the library for a screening of GOAT movie. The story follows Will, a small goat with big dreams who gets a once-in-a-lifetime shot to join the pros and play roarball--a high-intensity, co-ed, full-contact sport dominated by the fastest, fiercest animals in the world. Will's new teammates aren't thrilled about having a little goat on their roster, but Will is determined to revolutionize the sport and prove once and for all that 'smalls can ball! Drop in.

events.title: Sew Felt Animal Friends

This is a chance for families to work together on a simple sewing project and leave with a homemade toy or decorative piece.  Registration is required. events.title: Solar System Stories: A Merril Collection Exhibit
events.description: Take a trip around our solar system without leaving the comfort of planet Earth. Explore the strange landscapes of the inner and outer planets and their moons. We've even included Pluto! Learn how our understanding of these worlds has evolved since their discovery. In our exhibit you'll find original art, novels, short stories and more set on these fantastic celestial bodies. This exhibit can be viewed during the Merril Collection's regular hours. events.title: Harry Turns 70! Celebrating Canadian Illustrators
events.description: Harry the Dirty Dog turns 70! Celebrate 70 years of a beloved classic and the illustrations behind it. This exhibit highlights the work of Margaret Bloy Graham and her ability to bring stories to life through her artwork. Visit the Osborne collection to experience our exhibit, which also spotlights the contributions of Canadian illustrators, featuring works from artists all across the country. events.title: Scavenger Hunt: Local Ducks

This month's scavenger hunt features illustrations of local Toronto duck species from the Special Collections in the Arts at the Toronto Reference Library, many of which can be spotted in their full feathered glory on Toronto's waterfront.

events.title: Philippe Flahaut: Sous le soleil de l'été
events.description: [English version follows] Il s’agit d’un programme de chansons traditionnelles de France et du Québec, dont le vocabulaire est axé sur les activités estivales : les vacances, l’eau, le soleil, les animaux et les couleurs. events.title: Hands-On Dinosaur Fossils & Ancient Life
events.description: Join us for an opportunity to handle, examine up close and discuss nine real and replica fossils in this fascinating hands-on workshop. Participants will be entertained and educated, not just about the fossils themselves, but the paleontological aspects that underpin them. Concepts include: evolution, fossilization, ‘The Tree of Life’ (phylogeny) and — of course — dinosaurs! Presented by Marlowe Andreyko from Dino Bites. This program is for ages 9+ .  Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: STEM Sundays
events.description: Explore a different STEM-themed activity every Sunday the library is open. Topics include robotics, circuitry, building and more! For children aged 6-12 and their caregivers. Drop in.

events.title: Snap Circuits
events.description: Explore electricity and circuitry during this hands-on program. Ages 6-12. Drop-in.

events.title: Sunday STEM
events.description: Join us each week for a fun afternoon of exploring Science, Technology, Engineering and Math through small projects. Ages 6-12. Drop-in program, no registration required.

events.title: Sunday Funday
events.description: Join us for a fun afternoon of video games, board games, Lego and ping pong. Drop in, no registration required. Recommended for ages 6-12.

events.title: Sunday Family Crafts
events.description: Come as a family and enjoy making crafts together. This program is for families or caregivers with children. Drop-in. No registration required.

events.title: Sunday Fun-Day
events.description: **NEW TIME** The start time has changed for this event. It will now start at 3pm. Join us on Sunday afternoons for a variety of activities including STEM, arts & crafts and games. For children ages 6-12. Drop-in. Space and supplies are limited.

events.title: After School Club
events.description: Looking for something to do after school? Come out to our After School Club and learn new things, make stuff, meet other kids and have fun! For ages [6-12]. events.title: Story Planet Comic Lab
events.description: The Artists in the Library series comes to Evelyn Gregory Library this Fall!  Led by professional illustrators Hilary Leung and Joseph Bonsu, children will explore the art of comics and build a portfolio of work culminating in a polished long-form comic. The program concludes with a Comic Con showcase, where their work is shared with the community. For ages 9-12. Spaces are limited.  Registration required.

events.description: Join us on Saturday mornings and build to your heart’s content!  LEGO, DUPLO, and KEVA planks will be provided for you to explore your creativity. All children welcome with their caregivers. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Family Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs, rhymes, and more for children aged 0-5 with their families and/or caregivers. Older siblings are welcome! Drop-in; no registration required. Family Time occurs seasonally from October to March.

events.title: Robotics Workshops for Youth
events.description: Join Upper Toronto Community Robotics for accessible, hands-on robotics and engineering workshops for youth! These programs will help you build foundational skills in engineering design, safety, CAD software, 3D modeling, and introductory robotics coding. This month's theme: Control Systems This program is for youth in grades 6-12. It is highly recommended to attend each session to build on your learning. Please register online.

Drop-in program, no registration required.

events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Looking at archival images and current references, participants will be guided to bring their own stories into our city’s images using material such as watercolours, crayons, playdough, text, etc onto colouring pages. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Soundscape
events.description: Next we will be inspired by the library’s architecture and happenings as we are guided by musician Aniqa Qadir to create a soundscape. Working in groups, visitors will be prompted to absorb the sounds of the building and led through the process of creating an audio journey. This playful session welcomes participants to break the whisper norm of a library and interrupt the space with their observations. Led by Aniqa Qadir and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Raíces — Classical & Latin American Guitar
events.description: Toronto-based guitarist Karla García invites audiences on a musical journey from the elegance of the Baroque era to the rich musical traditions of Latin America. Featuring works by Bach, Dilermando Reis, Víctor Jara, Luiz Bonfá, and Chilean composers Antonio Restucci, alongside original compositions from her upcoming debut album, this concert celebrates the timeless voice of the classical guitar—intimate, soulful, and deeply human.

events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Tuesday to play board games with your friends! Ask us for our list of board games, and check our monthly calendar for Tournaments with prizes! All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Game Café
events.description: Drop-in every first Tuesday of the month to take part in video games and board games! Xbox One S games available. All games available on a first come, first serve basis.

events.title: Youth Hub: Study Hall Wednesdays
events.description: On Wednesdays, the Eatonville Youth Hub becomes a quiet space for teens to study, work on projects, and request homework help and resume help from staff. Bring your homework and enjoy a snack in a space that is just for teens. This program is part of Youth Hub service.
